summary: Phase 46 Shiprocket Integration Port - All 23 backend tests passed. Frontend Order Detail page verified with delivered order showing all 5 timeline stages filled, AWB chip, Track shipment link, and courier name (Bluedart).
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test data. Test order reset to confirmed status before each webhook test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 46 Shiprocket Integration fully tested. Key features: (1) Webhook endpoints at /api/shiprocket/webhooks/tracking and /api/shiprocket/webhooks/order-status receive Shiprocket events and update orders collection. (2) Status mapping: Pickup Scheduled→processing, Picked Up/In Transit/Out for Delivery→shipped, Delivered→delivered, RTO Initiated→cancelled. (3) Regression guard prevents delivered orders from regressing to earlier statuses. (4) Audit logging to shiprocket_events collection with raw_status and mapped_status. (5) Graceful handling of malformed payloads (returns 200 to avoid Shiprocket retry storms). (6) Frontend Order Detail page shows timeline, AWB chip, Track shipment link when order has awb_code.

tests: GET /api/shiprocket/webhooks/events returns 200 with ring buffer schema
Delivered order does not regress to shipped on In Transit webhook
Delivered order does not regress to processing on Pickup Scheduled webhook
Webhook creates audit entry in shiprocket_events with raw_status and mapped_status
Multiple webhooks create multiple audit entries
Unknown AWB returns 200 (logs warning, no crash)
GET /api/shiprocket/orders/ (live API smoke test) - no Python exception
Order Detail page loads with order number ORD-BGFIJQ
Timeline shows all 5 stages (Payment, Paid, Processing, Shipped, Delivered)
All timeline stages filled green for delivered order
